m365-068.attackPerspective: Power BI reports often contain aggregated sensitive data from multiple sources — financial reports, HR analytics, customer databases. Unrestricted external sharing means any user can share a dashboard link with anyone outside the organization, bypassing DLP controls. Attackers who compromise a Power BI user can exfiltrate entire data models, not just individual records.
m365-068.implementationNotes:
# Power BI Admin PowerShell
Connect-PowerBIServiceAccount

# Check current tenant settings
Get-PowerBITenantSetting | Where-Object { $_.SettingName -like '*external*' -or $_.SettingName -like '*share*' } | Select SettingName, Enabled | Format-Table

# Via Power BI Admin Portal:
# 1. Go to Admin Portal > Tenant Settings
# 2. Under 'Export and sharing settings':
#    - 'Allow Azure Active Directory guest users to access Power BI' = Specific security groups only
#    - 'Invite external users to your organization' = Disabled or specific groups
#    - 'Allow users to send Power BI content to external users' = Disabled
#    - 'Publish to web' = Disabled (this creates ANONYMOUS public links)

# Block 'Publish to web' (most critical)
# This setting creates completely public, unauthenticated links to reports
Set-PowerBITenantSetting -SettingName 'PublishToWeb' -Enabled $false
m365-068.validationNotes:
# Check for existing 'Publish to web' embed codes (anonymous access!)
Get-PowerBIPublishToWebLink | Select ReportName, CreatedBy, Status | Format-Table
# Expected: No active links (or only approved ones)

# Verify external sharing settings
Get-PowerBITenantSetting -SettingName 'AllowExternalSharingOfContent' | Select Enabled
m365-068.tuningNotes: Publish to web is the highest risk — it creates completely anonymous public URLs. Disable it org-wide first. For external sharing with authenticated guests, use specific security groups and require MFA via Conditional Access. Monitor Power BI activity logs for exports to CSV/Excel which bypass sharing controls.
m365-068.advancedDetection:
// KQL: Detect Power BI external sharing and exports
PowerBIActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Activity in ('ShareReport', 'ExportReport', 'PublishToWebReport', 'CreateGateway')
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, Activity, ReportName, DatasetName, WorkspaceName
| extend IsExternal = Activity has 'External' or Activity has 'PublishToWeb'
| sort by TimeGenerated desc

m365-069.attackPerspective: Without sensitivity labels on Power BI content, there's no way to enforce encryption or access controls on exported data. A user can export a 'Highly Confidential' dataset to a CSV file that has zero protection. Labels carry the protection with the data, even outside of Power BI.
m365-069.implementationNotes:
# Enable sensitivity labels for Power BI in Admin Portal
# Admin Portal > Tenant Settings > Information Protection:
#   'Allow users to apply sensitivity labels for Power BI content' = Enabled
#   'Apply sensitivity labels from data sources to their data in Power BI' = Enabled

# Via PowerShell (Graph API)
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Policy.ReadWrite.ConditionalAccess'

# Verify labels are available in Power BI
Get-PowerBITenantSetting -SettingName 'SensitivityLabelsForPBIContent' | Select Enabled

# Check which workspaces have unlabeled content
$workspaces = Get-PowerBIWorkspace -Scope Organization -All
foreach ($ws in $workspaces) {
  $reports = Get-PowerBIReport -WorkspaceId $ws.Id
  $unlabeled = $reports | Where-Object { -not $_.SensitivityLabel }
  if ($unlabeled) {
    Write-Warning "Workspace '$($ws.Name)' has $($unlabeled.Count) unlabeled reports"
  }
}
m365-069.validationNotes:
# Verify setting is enabled
Get-PowerBITenantSetting -SettingName 'SensitivityLabelsForPBIContent'
# Expected: Enabled = True

# Check label adoption
$reports = Get-PowerBIReport -Scope Organization
$labeled = ($reports | Where-Object { $_.SensitivityLabel }).Count
Write-Host "Labeled: $labeled / $($reports.Count) reports"
m365-069.tuningNotes: Start with enabling labels without mandatory labeling. Use auto-labeling for Power BI datasets connected to sensitive SQL tables or SharePoint sites. Mandatory labeling should be rolled out after users are trained and labels are standardized.
m365-069.advancedDetection:
// KQL: Detect unlabeled Power BI artifacts being shared
PowerBIActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Activity in ('ShareReport', 'ShareDashboard')
| where SensitivityLabelId == ''
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, Activity, ReportName, WorkspaceName
